description: This 3D surface plot (Fig. 3) illustrates the variation of the stress softening coefficient $k_s(\varepsilon+\Delta\varepsilon)$ for low-alloy steel under multi-stage hot deformation as a function of the natural logarithm of the Zener-Hollomon parameter of the second stage, $\ln Z_P^{(II)}(\varepsilon+\Delta\varepsilon)$, and the first-stage dynamic recrystallization (DRX) fraction $\varphi_d^{(I)}$. Four horizontal planes correspond to different levels of $\varphi_d^{(I)}$ ranging from approximately 0.1 at the bottom to 0.9 at the top. The color scale indicates $k_s$ values from ~0.2 (blue) to 1.0 (yellow). The figure reveals that $k_s$ declines with increasing difference in $Z_P$ between the two stages and with rising $\varphi_d^{(I)}$. At low first-stage DRX fractions, $k_s$ remains near unity across the $\ln Z_P$ range, while at higher $\varphi_d^{(I)}$ the coefficient drops substantially under divergent inter-stage deformation conditions. This supports the core finding that the saturation-stress softening effect intensifies with greater disparity in thermomechanical parameters and increased prior DRX development during transient deformation.
